vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially inaccurate. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/unidentifiable. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are present in the yellow-rimmed pot. P3 (Pothos) is present with the white rabbit anchor. A significant amount of organic debris (eggshells) has been introduced.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No evidence of String of Nickels.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos leaf visible with rabbit anchor.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Present: Shared in the yellow-rimmed pot.
inventory_reconciliation.New_Intervention: Eggshell fragments observed in the P3 pot.

plant_audit.P1: Not detected. Soil surface appears barren.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Stable. The succulent foliage in the yellow pot remains consistent throughout the sequence.
plant_audit.P3: The Pothos leaf shows signs of chlorosis or dehydration; the surrounding soil contains non-botanical debris (eggshells).

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears consistently moist/dark; no signs of surface cracking.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments are present in the P3 pot, likely an experimental calcium amendment or composting intervention.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Pothos leaf in P3 shows slight postural drooping.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_Current: Increased accumulation of eggshell debris in P3; Pothos leaf remains in a static, slightly wilted state.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The Pothos (P3) is under moderate stress, likely due to substrate compaction or nutrient imbalance indicated by the eggshell intervention. The succulent cluster (P2/P4) appears robust and acclimated to the current light levels.
anomalies: Eggshell fragments in P3 pot (New Intervention).
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently dominated by a single Pothos leaf and a cluster of succulents. The most notable development is the introduction of eggshells into the P3 pot, which suggests an attempt at soil enrichment. The Pothos leaf is showing signs of fatigue, while the succulents remain stable. P1 is effectively missing from the observed area.
